rejected_candidates.place_name: Durham Farmers Market / Durham Central Park Pavilion
rejected_candidates.rejection_reason: Market/pavilion use is primarily outdoor or open-air. No separable indoor public capture zone was source-backed for this lane.

Source evidence frames Boxyard as repurposed shipping containers around an outdoor entertainment/market setting. Individual tenant interiors may be separate future candidates, but the campus-wide record is not indoor-primary.
Campus-wide record is a mixed outdoor/office/restaurant district. It can only survive as a specific indoor tenant/lobby/retail corridor candidate with source-backed public access.
Fetched public evidence did not establish a zone-specific indoor public/common-access capture area separate from shops/restaurants and outdoor district circulation.
Campus-wide candidate is mixed and not indoor-zone-specific. A specific gallery or tenant interior can be researched as a separate candidate if source-backed.
Outdoor plaza/streetscape; no indoor allowed capture zone.
Historic indoor mall evidence is stale and current public indoor mall operations were not verified. Do not seed as live supply.
Venue rules prohibit video/audio recording devices. Do not promote through autonomous public-space lane.

Indoor public building, but the library filming/photography policy requires written permission for commercial/professional filming and restricts capturing the public/staff in backgrounds. Keep out of auto-promotion.
